headline: OpenAI Closes Record $122 Billion Funding Round at $852 Billion Valuation
raw_snippet: OpenAI closed its largest-ever funding round, raising $122 billion at an $852 billion post-money valuation. The round was anchored by Amazon ($50B), Nvidia and SoftBank ($30B each), with participation from a16z, D. E. Shaw Ventures, MGX, TPG, T. Rowe Price, and Microsoft. $35B of Amazon's commitment is contingent on OpenAI reaching IPO or AGI.
curator_notes: Primary funding announcement. Clustered with retail-investor angle.

headline: OpenAI Raises $3 Billion From Retail Investors in Largest Private Placement Ever by Major Banks
raw_snippet: As part of the $122B round, OpenAI raised $3 billion from individual investors via private placements through JP Morgan, Morgan Stanley, and Goldman Sachs — tripling its original $1B retail target and marking the largest private placement ever handled by those banks. OpenAI shares will be added to several ARK Invest ETFs, blurring the line between private and public fundraising ahead of an expected 2026 IPO.

headline: OpenAI Discloses $2 Billion Monthly Revenue Run Rate and 900 Million Weekly Active Users
raw_snippet: Alongside the record funding round, OpenAI disclosed it is generating $2 billion in monthly revenue with more than 900 million ChatGPT weekly active users and 50+ million paid subscribers. Enterprise now represents >40% of OpenAI's revenue and is on track to reach parity with consumer by year-end. CFO Sarah Friar said the company is growing revenue 4x faster than Alphabet and Meta at comparable stages.
curator_notes: Critical operating metrics for IB audience.

headline: OpenAI Acquires TBPN, First Media Company Acquisition, to Expand Tech-Industry Voice
raw_snippet: OpenAI announced the acquisition of Technology Business Programming Network (TBPN), the daily live tech talk show hosted by Jordi Hays and John Coogan, marking OpenAI's first acquisition of a media company. TBPN will maintain editorial independence but report to OpenAI's chief political operative Chris Lehane. The show is on track to pull in more than $30 million in annual revenue per the Wall Street Journal.
curator_notes: First media acquisition; clustered with IPO-context angle.

headline: OpenAI Acquires TBPN, Raising IPO-Era Questions Over Media Independence
raw_snippet: OpenAI's purchase of founder-led tech talk show TBPN formalizes an unusual media-company acquisition by a frontier AI lab. TechCrunch notes the deal fits a broader pattern of OpenAI making strategic M&A moves ahead of an expected 2026 IPO, following the Astral, Statsig, and io hardware deals over the past year.

headline: OpenAI Launches Pay-As-You-Go Codex Seats for ChatGPT Business and Enterprise
raw_snippet: OpenAI introduced Codex-only seats on pay-as-you-go pricing for ChatGPT Business and Enterprise. The new tier has no rate limits and is billed per-token via a new credit-based rate card matching API pricing. Usage on Codex within ChatGPT Business has grown 6x since January, with more than 2 million weekly Codex users overall.
curator_notes: Tightly clustered with the $20/seat price cut.

headline: OpenAI Cuts ChatGPT Business Annual Seat Price by $5/Month to $20
raw_snippet: OpenAI lowered the annual price of ChatGPT Business subscriptions by $5/month, bringing the per-seat price down to $20/month. The price cut comes alongside the Codex pay-as-you-go launch and is positioned as a competitive response to enterprise AI rivals.

headline: ChatGPT Adds Hands-Free CarPlay Voice Chat for Supported iOS Vehicles
raw_snippet: OpenAI added hands-free CarPlay voice chat support, allowing users on iOS 26.4+ to start and resume ChatGPT voice conversations directly from the CarPlay interface in supported vehicles. The rollout positions ChatGPT as a direct voice competitor to in-car assistants from Apple, Google, and Amazon.

headline: OpenAI Completes GPT-4o Retirement, Ending Phased Sunset of Legacy Frontier Model
raw_snippet: OpenAI completed the full retirement of GPT-4o across all ChatGPT plans on April 3, closing out a phased sunset that began February 13, 2026. The move consolidates consumer tiers on GPT-5.4 as OpenAI prepares for the forthcoming GPT-5.5 release.

headline: OpenAI Announces Retirement of Standard Voice Mode Within 30 Days
raw_snippet: OpenAI said it will retire Standard Voice Mode in 30 days, unifying all ChatGPT users onto Advanced Voice Mode. Plus users get near-unlimited access; Free users get several hours per day.
curator_notes: Notable product sunset with workflow impact for paid users.

headline: OpenAI Product & Business Chief Fidji Simo Takes Multi-Week Medical Leave
raw_snippet: Fidji Simo, OpenAI's CEO of Applications, told staff on Friday she is taking several weeks of medical leave to manage a worsening neuroimmune condition (POTS). President Greg Brockman will oversee product — including the new desktop super app project — in her absence.
curator_notes: Cluster of 4 distinct executive changes on the same day. Primary item describes Simo's leave; the rest are clustered.

headline: OpenAI COO Brad Lightcap Moves to New 'Special Projects' Role Reporting to Sam Altman
raw_snippet: As part of a broader April 3 leadership reshuffle, OpenAI COO Brad Lightcap transitioned out of his operational role and into a new 'special projects' mandate focused on complex deals and investments. He now reports directly to CEO Sam Altman. Denise Dresser, former Slack CEO and OpenAI's recently hired Chief Revenue Officer, assumes most of Lightcap's former commercial responsibilities.
cut_reason: Clustered with Simo medical leave (primary executive reshuffle item).

headline: OpenAI CMO Kate Rouch Steps Down to Focus on Breast Cancer Recovery
raw_snippet: OpenAI Chief Marketing Officer Kate Rouch, the company's first CMO and a former Coinbase and Meta marketing executive, stepped down from her role to focus on late-stage breast cancer recovery. She said she may return in a more narrowly scoped role. OpenAI is now searching for a new marketing head.
cut_reason: Clustered with Simo medical leave (primary executive reshuffle item).

headline: Former Slack CEO Denise Dresser Takes Over OpenAI Commercial Operations
raw_snippet: Denise Dresser, former Slack CEO who joined OpenAI as Chief Revenue Officer earlier this year, assumed most of Brad Lightcap's former commercial duties during the April 3 leadership reshuffle. The move puts a seasoned enterprise SaaS operator in charge of OpenAI's revenue engine as it pushes toward parity between enterprise and consumer revenue.
cut_reason: Clustered with Simo medical leave (primary executive reshuffle item).

headline: OpenAI Secondary Market Demand Sinks to $765B Valuation Even as Primary Round Hits $852B
raw_snippet: OpenAI shares are trading on secondary markets at an implied ~$765 billion valuation — roughly a 10% discount to the $852 billion price set in the March 31 primary round — as institutional investors pivot to Anthropic. Morgan Stanley and Goldman Sachs have begun offering OpenAI shares to wealth clients without carry fees, while secondary marketplace Next Round Capital said it could not find buyers for $600 million of OpenAI shares from ~six institutions looking to sell. Anthropic is seeing record demand on Augment and Hiive.
curator_notes: Important price-discovery signal — secondary trades at 10% discount to primary round.

headline: Fidji Simo Drove OpenAI to Shutter Sora, Steer Clear of Social Media Products Ahead of Medical Leave
raw_snippet: Per The Information, OpenAI's Fidji Simo spearheaded both the TBPN acquisition and the decision to shut down Sora, pushing OpenAI to avoid building consumer social media products and instead lean into media-distribution partnerships. The reporting contextualizes the TBPN deal as part of a deliberate strategic pivot away from social-first product bets.
cut_reason: Interpretive context piece on a previous-week event (Sora shutdown). Not a new event.

headline: CFO Sarah Friar Warns 2026 IPO Timeline Too Aggressive, Reportedly at Odds with Altman
raw_snippet: OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar has reportedly raised internal concerns that the company's Q4 2026 IPO timeline is too aggressive given its $600 billion in cloud capacity commitments and slowing revenue growth. Friar is reportedly at odds with CEO Sam Altman over the timing and was subsequently shifted to report to Fidji Simo instead of Altman directly.
curator_notes: Internal executive tension on IPO timing. Single-source but newsworthy.

headline: OpenAI Launches Visual Product Discovery in ChatGPT via Expanded Agentic Commerce Protocol
raw_snippet: OpenAI launched richer visual product discovery in ChatGPT through its expanded Agentic Commerce Protocol (ACP). Users can browse products visually, compare options side-by-side with pricing and reviews, and upload images to find similar items. Retailers including Target, Sephora, Nordstrom, Best Buy, The Home Depot, and Wayfair have integrated into ACP. The feature is rolling out across all ChatGPT tiers.
curator_notes: Major commerce expansion with national retailers.

headline: OpenAI Publishes 'Inside Our Approach to the Model Spec' Detailing AI Behavior Framework
raw_snippet: OpenAI published a detailed explanation of its Model Spec — the public framework that defines how OpenAI's models adjudicate between obligations to users, developers, and OpenAI itself. The post details the new authority hierarchy (Root > System > Developer > User > Guideline) and updated principles for agentic actions and teen-user safety.
curator_notes: Important framework document for AI policy/compliance discussion.

headline: Federal Judge Affirms Order Requiring OpenAI to Release 20 Million Anonymized ChatGPT Logs in Copyright Case
raw_snippet: US District Judge Sidney Stein affirmed a magistrate judge's order requiring OpenAI to produce 20 million anonymized ChatGPT conversation logs in the consolidated AI copyright litigation brought by The New York Times, Chicago Tribune, and other publishers. The court rejected OpenAI's privacy objections, finding the logs relevant to plaintiffs' fair-use analysis even where they do not directly reproduce copyrighted works.
